lesson.menu_desc: Modals: should / must / have to / can't / ought to -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 8 -- Advice & Rules -- Modals (should / must / have to / can't / ought to)
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Sandra Hayasaki | Aula 8 -- Advice & Rules

listenings.text: Let me give you some advice about working from home, because I made a lot of mistakes when I started. First, you should set clear working hours. In my first month, I was answering emails at midnight, and I quickly felt tired all the time. You really ought to protect your evenings. Second, you must take real breaks. You can't sit at your desk for eight hours and expect to think clearly. I recommend a short walk after lunch. Third, if you have a deadline, you have to tell your family early, so they can support you. And one last suggestion: avoid working in your pajamas. It sounds funny, but getting dressed helps your brain understand that the workday has started. These small rules changed everything for me.

I want to recommend something that really helped me this year: joining a morning exercise class. If you want to start, here is my advice. You don't have to be fit already; everyone is a beginner at the start. You should choose a class near your home, because you must make it easy to go, or you will find excuses. The gym has a few simple rules: you have to bring your own towel, and you can't use your phone during the class. One suggestion I always give: you ought to drink water before and after, not during. And please, avoid comparing yourself to other people. The only rule that really matters is that you should be a little better than you were yesterday.

extra_audio.text: First, say what the problem is. Then give your main piece of advice with should or ought to. Next, explain the rule they must follow or the thing they have to do. After that, warn them about what they can't do or should avoid. Finally, recommend one small action to start with.
